title: API Request Simulator
description: A Postman-style API sandbox with CRUD RESTful operations against an in-memory mock database, JSON body validation with CSS shake animation, syntax-highlighted JSON response viewer, async latency simulation, custom headers grid, and scrolling request history log.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: In-memory CRUD engine: db.products array with {id,title,price,stock}. Handlers for GET (collection or /:id), POST (JSON.parse validation + id auto-increment via nextId), PUT (id-targeted full replacement), DELETE (splice removal with 404 if missing)
Endpoint parser: strips /api/v1 prefix, splits into collection/id, validates against collections array — returns {collection, id} or {err} for unknown routes
JSON validation: try/catch around JSON.parse(bodyText) — on failure, CSS shake animation on textarea + status set to '400 Bad Request' with error overlay, no further processing
Async latency simulation: delay(ms) returning Promise with setTimeout, random 150–600ms per request to mimic server processing. Latency displayed in telemetry as 'Xms'

Syntax-highlighted JSON viewer: lightweight highlighter wrapping JSON.stringify(…,null,2) with regex replacements — keys in cyan (class 'key'), strings in green ('str'), numbers in pink ('num'), booleans in gold ('bool'), null in gray, brackets in muted white
Custom headers grid: dynamic key-value rows added via '+ HEADER' button, each row two inputs (Key/Value), user-extendable
Request history: per-request entries appended to scrolling log panel showing method (color-coded), endpoint, and HTTP status — GET=cyan, POST=green, PUT=amber, DELETE=magenta
Color-coded method selector: CSS class toggles on select element for border/text color (GET=#00f0ff, POST=#00ff66, PUT=#ffd700, DELETE=#ff2a5f)
Reset Server State: restores db.products to original 5 items + resets nextId to 6, clears response panel without page reload
